Tests string replacement using regular expressions in JavaScript.

# Purpose
This code is a short script that tests the behavior of the `String.prototype.replace` method in JavaScript. It uses regular expressions to replace patterns at the start and end of an empty string. The first `assert` statement checks if replacing the end of an empty string (`/$/g`) with the character "a" results in the string "a". The second `assert` statement verifies if replacing the start of an empty string (`/^/g`) with "a" also results in "a". These assertions confirm that the `replace` method correctly handles regular expressions for empty strings.
